verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things have I spoken unto you, that My joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is My commandment, that ye love one another, as I have loved you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All things good then have their reward, when they arrive at their proper end, but if they be cut off midway, shipwreck ensues.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as a vessel of immense burden, if it reach not the harbor in time, but founder in the midst of the sea, gains nothing from the length of the voyage, but even makes the calamity greater, in proportion as it has endured more toils; so are those souls which fall back when near the end of their labors, and faint in the midst of the struggle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul said, that glory, and honor, and peace, should meet those who ran their course with patient continuance in well-doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A thing which Christ now effecteth in the case of the disciples. ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He had accepted them, and they rejoiced in Him, and then the sudden coming of the Passion and His sad words were likely to cut short their pleasure; after having conversed with them sufficiently to soothe them, He addeth, “These things have I spoken unto you, that My joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be fulfilled”; that is, “that ye might not be separated from Me, that ye might not cut short your course.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye were rejoicing in Me, and ye were rejoicing exceedingly, but despondency hath fallen upon you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then I remove, that joy may come at the last, showing that your present circumstances are fit cause, not for pain, but for pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I saw you offended; I despised you not; I said not, ‘Why do ye not continue noble?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I spake to you words which brought comfort with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so I wish ever to keep you in the same love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye have heard concerning a kingdom, ye rejoiced.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order therefore that your joy might be fulfilled, I have spoken these things unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But “this is the commandment, that ye love one another as I have loved you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the love of God is intertwined with our own, and connected like a sort of chain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it sometimes saith that there are two commandments, sometimes only one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is not possible that the man who hath taken hold on the first should not possess the second also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For at one time He said, “On this the Law and the Prophets hang” ( Matt. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 40 ); and at another, “Whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, “Love is the fulfilling of the Law.” ( Rom. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which He saith also here; for if to abide proceeds from love, and love from the keeping of the commandments, and the commandment is that we love one another, then the abiding in God proceeds from love towards each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He doth not simply speak of love, but declareth also the manner, “As I have loved you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He showeth, that His very departure was not of hatred but of love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So that I ought rather to be admired on this account, for I lay down My life for you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet nowhere doth He say this in these words, but in a former place, by sketching the best shepherd, and here by exhorting them, and by showing the greatness of His love, and Himself, who He is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He everywhere exalt love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because this is the mark of the disciples, this the bond of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account Paul saith such great things of it, as being a genuine disciple of Christ, and having had experience of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye are My friends —henceforth I call you not servants, for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye are My friends, for all things which I have heard of My Father I have made known unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He, “I have many things to tell you, but ye cannot bear them now”? ( c. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the “all” and the “hearing” He showeth nothing else, but that He uttered nothing alien, but only what was of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since to speak of secrets appears to be the strongest proof of friendship, “ye have,” He saith, “been deemed worthy even of this communion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When however He saith “all,” He meaneth, “whatever things it was fit that they should hear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He putteth also another sure proof of friendship, no common one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of what sort was that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have not chosen Me, but I have chosen you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, I ran upon your friendship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He stayed not here, but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I set you,” He saith, (that is, “I planted you,”) “that ye should go,” (He still useth the metaphor of the vine,) that is, “that ye should extend yourselves”; “and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now if your fruit remain, much more shall ye.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I have not only loved you,” He saith, “but have done you the greatest benefits, by extending your branches through all the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou in how many ways He showeth His love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By telling them things secret, by having in the first instance run to meet their friendship, by granting them the greatest blessings, by suffering for them what then He suffered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After this, He showeth that He also remaineth continually with those who shall bring forth fruit; for it is needful to enjoy His aid, and so to bear fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in My Name, He may give it you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet it is the part of the person asked to do the thing asked; but if the Father is asked, how is it that the Son doeth it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is that thou mayest learn that the Son is not inferior to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things I command you, that ye love one another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “It is not to upbraid, that I tell you that I lay down My life for you, or that I ran to meet you, but in order to lead you into friendship.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, since the being persecuted and insulted by the many, was a grievous and intolerable thing, and enough to humble even a lofty soul, therefore, after having said ten thousand things first, Christ entered upon this matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having first smoothed their minds, He thus proceedeth to these points, showing that these things too were for their exceeding advantage, as He had also shown that the others were.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as He had told them that they ought not to grieve, but rather to rejoice, “because I go to the Father,” (since He did this not as deserting but as greatly loving them,) so here also He showeth that they ought to rejoice, not grieve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how He effecteth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “I know that the action is grievous, but bear for My sake, since for My sake also ye suffer,” for this reason was not yet sufficient to console them; wherefore letting this pass, He putteth forward another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is that this thing would be a sure proof of their former virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And, on the contrary, ye ought to grieve, not because ye are hated now but if ye were likely to be loved”; for this He implieth by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye were of the world, the world would love its own.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that had ye been loved it would be very clear that ye had shown forth signs of wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, when by saying this first, He did not effect his purpose, He goeth on again with the discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The servant is not greater than his lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If they have persecuted Me, they will also persecute you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showed that in this point they would be most His imitators.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For while Christ was in the flesh, men had war with Him, but when He was translated, the battle came in the next place upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then because owing to their fewness they were terrified at being about to encounter the attack of so great a multitude, He raiseth their souls by telling them that it was an especial subject of joy that they were hated by them; “For so ye shall share My sufferings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye should not therefore be troubled, for ye are not better than I,” as I before told you, “The servant is not greater than his lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then there is also a third source of consolation, that the Father also is insulted together with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But all these things will they do unto you for My Name’s sake, because they know not Him that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “they insult Him also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides this, depriving those others of excuse, and putting also another source of comfort, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that they shall do unjustly both what they do against Him and against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then didst Thou bring us into such calamities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Didst Thou not foreknow the wars, the hatred?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore again He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that hateth Me, hateth My Father also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this also proclaiming beforehand no small punishment against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, since they continually pretended that they persecuted Him on account of the Father, to deprive them of this excuse He spake these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They have no excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I gave them the teaching which is by words, that by works I added, according to the Law of Moses, who bade all men obey one speaking and doing such things, when he should both lead to piety, and exhibit the greatest miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He spake not simply of “signs,” but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Which none other man did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And of this they themselves are witnesses, speaking in this way; “It was never so seen in Israel” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ); and, “Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 32 ); and the matter of Lazarus was of the same kind, and all the other acts the same, and the mode of wonder-working new, and all beyond thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then,” saith one, “do they persecute both Thee and us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because ye are not of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye were of the world, the world would love its own.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He first remindeth them of the words which He spake also to His own brethren ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ); but there he spake more by way of a reflection, lest He should offend them, while here, on the contrary, He revealed all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how is it clear that it is on this account that we are hated?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From what was done to Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, tell Me, which of My words or deeds could they lay hold on, that they would not receive Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then since the thing would be astounding to us, He telleth the cause; that is, their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He stayeth not here either, but introduceth the Prophet ( Ps. xxxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4 ), showing him proclaiming before of old time, and saying, that,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They hated Me without a cause.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which Paul doth also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when many wondered how that the Jews believed not, he brings in Prophets foretelling it of old, and declaring the cause; that their wickedness and pride were the cause of their unbelief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Well then; if they kept not Thy saying, neither will they keep ours; if they persecuted Thee, therefore they will persecute us also; if they saw signs, such as none other man wrought; if they heard words such as none other spake, and profited nothing; if they hate Thy Father and Thee with Him, wherefore,” saith one, “hast Thou sent us in among them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How after this shall we be worthy of belief? which of our kindred will give-heed to us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That they may not therefore be troubled by such thoughts, see what sort of comfort he addeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of Truth, which proceedeth from the Father, He shall testify of Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with Me from the beginning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He shall be worthy of belief, for He is the Spirit of Truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He called It not “Holy Spirit,” but “Spirit of Truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the, “proceedeth from the Father,” showeth that He knoweth all things exactly, as Christ also saith of Himself, that “I know whence come and whither I go” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 ), speaking in that place also concerning truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whom will send.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Behold, it is no longer the Father alone, but the Son also who sendeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And ye too,” He saith, “have a right to be believed, who have been with Me, who have not heard from others.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, the Apostles confidently rely on this circumstance, saying, “We who did eat and drink with Him.” ( Acts x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that this was not merely said to please, the Spirit beareth witness to the words spoken. ( Acts x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ch. xvi. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “when ye see many disbelieve, and yourselves ill-treated.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They shall put you out of the synagogues.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (For “the Jews had already agreed, that if any one should confess Christ, he should be put out of the synagogues”— c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They shall so seek after your murder, as of an action pious and pleasing to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then again He addeth the consolation,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And these things will they do, because they have not known the Father, nor Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It is sufficient for your comfort that ye endure these things for My sake, and the Father’s.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He remindeth them of the blessedness of which He spake at the beginning, “Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for My sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad; for great is your reward in heaven.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So, judging from these words, deem the rest also trustworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ye will not be able to say, that I flatteringly told you only those things which would please you, nor that the words were words of deceit; for one who intended to deceive, would not have told you beforehand of matters likely to turn you away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I have therefore told you before, that these things might not fall upon you unexpectedly, and trouble you; and for another reason besides, that ye might not say, that I did not foreknow that these things would be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Remember then that I have told you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And indeed the heathen always covered their persecutions of them by a pretense of their wickedness, driving them out as corrupters; but this did not trouble the disciples who had heard beforehand, and knew for what they suffered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The cause of what took place was sufficient to rouse their courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He everywhere handleth this, saying, “they have not known Me”; and, “for My sake they shall do it”; and, “for My Name’s sake, and for the Father’s sake”; and, “I suffered first”; and, “from no just cause they dare these things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us too consider these things in our temptations, when we suffer anything from wicked men, “looking to the Beginner and Finisher of our faith” ( Heb. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ), and considering that it is by wicked men, and that it is for virtue’s sake, and for His sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we reflect on these things, all will be most easy and tolerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if one suffering for those he loves is even proud of it, what feeling of things dreadful will he have who suffers for the sake of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if He, for our sake, calleth that shameful thing, the Cross, “glory” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31 ), much more ought we to be thus disposed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we can so despise sufferings, much more shall we be able to despise riches, and covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We ought then, when about to endure anything unpleasant, to think not of the toils but of the crowns; for as merchants take into account not the seas only, but also the profits, so ought we to reckon on heaven and confidence towards God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if the getting more seem a pleasant thing, think that Christ willeth it not, and straightway it will appear displeasing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if it be grievous to you to give to the poor, stay not your reckoning at the expense, but straightway transport your thoughts to the harvest which results from the sowing; and when it is hard to despise the love of a strange woman, think of the crown which comes after the struggle, and thou shalt easily bear the struggle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if fear diverts a man from unseemly things, much more should the love of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Difficult is virtue; but let us cast around her form the greatness of the promise of things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed those who are virtuous, even apart from these promises, see her beautiful in herself, and on this account go after her, and work because it seems good to God, not for hire; and they think it a great thing to be sober-minded, not in order that they may not be punished, but because God hath commanded it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if any one is too weak for this, let him think of the prizes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So let us do in respect of alms-doing, let us pity our fellow-men, let us not, I entreat, neglect them when perishing with hunger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How can it be otherwise than an unseemly thing, that we should sit at the table laughing and enjoying ourselves, and when we hear others wailing as they pass through the street, should not even turn at their cries, but be wroth with them, and call them “cheat”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What meanest thou, man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth any one plan a cheat for a single loaf of bread?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yes,” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then in this case above all let him be pitied; in this case above all let him be delivered from his need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if thou art not minded to give, do not insult either; if thou wilt not save the wreck, do not thrust it into the gulf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For consider, when thou thrustest away the poor man who comes to thee, who thou wilt be when thou callest upon God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “With what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider how he departs, crushed, bowed down, lamenting; besides his poverty having received also the blow from your insolence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if ye count the begging a curse, think what a tempest it makes, begging to get nothing, but to go away insulted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we be like wild beasts, and know not nature itself through greediness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many groan at these words; but I desire them not now, but always, to have this feeling of compassion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think, I pray you, of that day when we shall stand before the judgment-seat of Christ, when we shall beg for mercy, and Christ, bringing them forward, shall say, “For the sake of a single loaf, of a single obol, so great a surge did ye raise in these souls!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What shall we reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What defense shall we make?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that He will bring them forward, hear what He saith; “Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of these, ye did it not to Me.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They will no more say anything to us, but God on their behalf will upbraid us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since the rich man saw Lazarus too, and Lazarus said nothing to him, but Abraham spake for him; and thus it will be in the case of the poor who are now despised by us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We shall not see them stretching out their hands in pitiful state, but being in rest; and we shall take the state which was theirs (and would that it were that state only, and not one much more grievous) as a punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither did the rich man desire to be filled with crumbs “there,” but was scorched and tormented sharply, and was told, “Thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things.” ( Luke xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then deem wealth any great thing; it will help us on our way to punishment, if we take not heed, just as, if we take heed, poverty also becomes to us an addition of enjoyment and rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we both put off our sins if we bear it with thankfulness, and gain great boldness before God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then not be ever seeking security here, in order that we may enjoy security there; but let us accept the labors which are in behalf of virtue, and cut off superfluities, and seek nothing more than we need, and spend all our substance on those who want.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since what excuse can we have, when God promiseth heaven to us, and we will not even give Him bread? when He indeed for thee maketh the sun to rise, and supplieth all the ministry of the Creation, but thou dost not even give Him a garment, nor allow Him to share thy roof?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of sun and moon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath set His Body before thee, He hath given thee His Precious Blood; and dost thou not even impart to Him of thy cup?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But hast thou done so for once?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is not mercy; as long as, having the means, thou helpest not, thou hast not yet fulfilled the whole duty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus the virgins who had the lamps, had oil, but not in abundance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, thou oughtest, even didst thou give from thine own, not to be so miserly, but now when thou givest what is thy Lord’s, why countest thou every little?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will ye that I tell you the cause of this inhumanity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When men get together their wealth through greediness, these same are slow to give alms; for one who has learnt so to gain, knows not how to spend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how can a man prepared for rapine adapt himself to its contrary?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who takes from others, how shall he be able to give up his own to another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A dog accustomed to feed on flesh cannot guard the flock; therefore the shepherds kill such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That this be not our fate, let us refrain from such feasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these men too feed on flesh, when they bring on death by hunger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not how God hath allowed to us all things in common?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If amid riches He hath suffered men to be poor, it is for the consolation of the rich, that they may be able by showing mercy towards them to put off their sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But thou even in this hast been cruel and inhuman; whence it is evident, that if thou hadst received this same power in greater things, thou wouldest have committed ten thousand murders, and wouldest have debarred men from light, and from life altogether.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That this might not take place, necessity hath cut short insatiableness in such matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye are pained when ye hear these things, much more I when I see them taking place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shalt thou be rich, and that man poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Till evening, but no farther; for so short is life, and all things so near their end, and all things henceforth so stand at the door, that the whole must be deemed but a little hour.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What need hast thou of bursting storehouses, of a multitude of domestics and house-keepers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why hast thou not ten thousand proclaimers of thy almsdoing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The storehouse utters no voice, yet will it bring upon thee many robbers; but the storehouses of the poor will go up to God Himself, and will make thy present life sweet, and put away all thy sins, and thou shalt gain glory from God, and honor from men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then grudgest thou thyself such good things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For thou wilt not do so much good to the poor, as to thyself, when thou benefitest them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt right their present state; but for thyself thou wilt lay up beforehand the glory and confidence which shall be hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this may we all obtain, by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be the glory and the might for ever.
